title: Banksia dallanneyi
intro: Banksia dallanneyi, also known as Dallachy's banksia, is a species of shrub in the genus Banksia, native to the coastal regions of New South Wales and Victoria in Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in Aboriginal medicine and is valued for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are said to aid in wound healing and reduce the risk of infections. The bioactive constituents of Banksia dallanneyi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infused oil, and is also used in topical applications to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Banksia dallanneyi is a species of flowering plant in the family Proteaceae. It is endemic to Western Australia and is named after botanist Dr. Nancye Dallanney. The species was first described in 2002 by Austin Mast and Kevin Thiele. Banksia dallanneyi is a shrub that grows up to 1.5 meters in height and is characterized by its conical inflorescences and yellow or yellow-brown flowers. It is considered a rare and endangered species due to the destruction of its habitat.
